Pass base_path output to build step as NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH

configure-pages patches next.config.js directly (only supports .js/.cjs/.mjs,
not .ts) and never sets NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH as an env var. Our image path
code therefore always saw an empty string at build time.

Give the Setup Pages step an id and pipe its base_path output explicitly into
the Build step so process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH is correctly set to
/crispkatbe when next build runs.

commit.message:
Fix image paths for GitHub Pages sub-path deployment

The site is deployed at /crispkatbe/ on GitHub Pages, but images were
referenced as absolute paths (e.g. /images/blog/...) causing them to
resolve against the domain root instead of the sub-path.

- next.config.ts: read basePath from NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH env var,
  which is automatically set by actions/configure-pages@v5
- lib/posts.ts: prefix local src paths in remark-rendered HTML with
  the base path at build time
- app/posts/[slug]/page.tsx: prefix coverImage src and OG image URL
  with the base path via a module-level withBasePath() helper

commit.message:
feat(capacity_scoreboard): separate parser trend tracking from contextual penalties

- Add parserCoreConfidence and parserContextPenalty fields to CapacityEntry typedef
- computeCapacityIndex now uses parserCoreConfidence for the parserQuality dimension
  so parser structural quality is tracked independently from contextual signals
  (bottleneckCoverage, architectureDrift) — falls back to parserConfidence for compat
- promptQuality continues to use aggregate parserConfidence (backward compatible)
- orchestrator passes parserCoreConfidence + parserContextPenalty from prometheusAnalysis
  to appendCapacityEntry so the split data is persisted to state/capacity_scoreboard.json
- computeTrend already supports any field — callers can now trend parserCoreConfidence
  separately from the aggregate parserConfidence
- Add 6 targeted tests covering the divergence between parser core and penalized aggregate

* fix(daemon): kill orphan daemons on start/stop; set trust-boundary=warn; planQuality configurable

- daemon_control: add killAllDaemonProcesses(), fix isDaemonProcess regex (cli.[jt]s)
- cli: sweep orphan daemons in boxOff(), boxOn(), and start command
- box.config.json: trustBoundaryMode=warn (no hard blocks), planQualityMinScore=0
- athena_reviewer: planQualityMinScore reads from config.runtime (falls back to 40)

* fix(agent_loader): auto-prune prompt files, keep last 3 per slug; restore cleanupPromptFile

* fix(logger): chatLog mirrors to live_worker_{slug}.log per speaker role

* test(quality): recalibrate parser confidence channels + governance BLOCK e2e assertions

Task 1 - Recalibrate parser core/context confidence composition:
- Add 8 new tests to prometheus_parse.test.ts covering the channel-split
  formula edge cases:
  * Combined core+context penalty: healthField/budget go to core channel,
    bottleneckCoverage goes to context channel — exact numeric verification
  * Floor-break behavior: large context penalty pushes aggregate to 0.1,
    documents when the core-context invariant intentionally breaks
  * Property invariant: parserConfidence is always >= 0.1 across all
    penalty combinations (5 input cases)
  * Channel invariant: holds exactly when floor is not triggered
    (uses 3 bottlenecks, 1 covered → coverage=0.33 < FLOOR=0.5)
  * Core floor: stacked penalties can't push parserCoreConfidence < 0.1
  * Channel routing: requestBudget penalty goes to core (not context)
  * Channel routing: healthField penalty goes to core (not context)
  * Rounding stability: all three channel values are always 2-decimal

Task 2 - Add governance BLOCK end-to-end flow assertions:
- Add 8 new tests to orchestrator_gate_precedence.test.ts covering
  decision envelope propagation:
  * Budget exhaustion gate: writes temp budget file (remainingUsd=0),
    verifies BUDGET_ELIGIBILITY fires with correct gateIndex
  * Carry-forward debt gate: writes temp ledger with 3 critical overdue
    entries, verifies CARRY_FORWARD_DEBT fires with correct gateIndex
  * Precedence ordering: freeze (idx=3) fires before drift (idx=7)
    when both conditions are simultaneously active
  * All blocked gate types carry non-null reason + numeric gateIndex >= 1
  * cycleId propagation: non-blocked result carries the argument cycleId
  * Evidence coupling reason: encodes the failing plan's task_id
  * Negative path: plan with valid evidence does NOT trigger coupling gate
  * Clean pass envelope: empty plans + disabled gates → all optional
    fields absent (no gateIndex, no mandatoryDriftPaths, etc.)

* feat(prometheus): use resolved canonical health in parser confidence scoring

Replace raw field-only presence check with 3-tier resolved canonical health
scoring in normalizePrometheusParsedOutput:

- healthField = 1.0: model provided an explicit canonical or alias value
  (no penalty, unchanged behaviour for valid inputs)
- healthField = 0.9: field was absent/unrecognized but projectHealth was
  successfully resolved via inferProjectHealth (lighter penalty
  health_field_inferred_from_text, delta -0.1 instead of -0.2)
- healthField = 0.8: unresolvable (defensive; currently unreachable because
  inferProjectHealth always returns a canonical default)

This ensures cycles where the LLM omits the projectHealth field but the
analysis text carries a recognisable health signal are not over-penalised.
All 3549 tests pass; zero new lint errors.

enemies (tank, ranged, healer, ambusher, scout) to improve player
recognizability. Each role now has a distinct silhouette identifiable
even at small sprite sizes (16-24px).

Role-specific visual changes:
- Tank: Wide shoulders, pauldrons, stocky build, helmet, large shield
- Ranged: Lean build, elongated arms, large weapon silhouette, bow string
- Healer: Robed appearance, glowing hands, medical cross symbol
- Ambusher: Crouched posture, pointed hood, concealed dagger
- Scout: Compact build, utility belt with pouches, backpack

Technical changes:
- Extended bodyPartPositions struct with silhouette modifiers
- calculateBodyPartPositions now applies role-specific proportions
- drawTorso includes shoulder width and pauldron rendering
- drawHead includes helmet and hood silhouettes with eyes
- drawWeapon uses weaponScale for role-appropriate sizing
- drawRoleSpecificDecorations adds visual markers for all 5 roles
- drawLegs uses stanceWidthMod for wider/narrower stances

All 5 genre variations (fantasy, scifi, horror, cyberpunk, postapoc)

commit.message:
Fix redraw ghost when closing About dialog (#72)

* Initial plan

* Fix ghost when closing About dialog via OK button

After show_dialog's event loop exits, post a fresh RefreshStencil
followed by invalidate_window for all visible windows, then flush with
repost_messages().

Root cause: destroy_window posts kWindowMessageRefreshStencil (sentinel
target = (window_t*)1) then queues paint messages for overlapping windows
via invalidate_overlaps.  The recursive clear_window_children call then
destroys each child window; each child's destroy_window re-posts
RefreshStencil, and the dedup logic nullifies the earlier one and
appends a new one *after* the already-queued paint messages.  So
repost_messages() inside the loop processes paint calls against a stale
stencil that still carries the dialog's window ID, and fill_rect for
the background windows is scissored out - leaving a visual ghost.

The X-button path calls end_dialog from dispatch_message at the top
level, so the same ordering issue exists, but dialog children receive
no extra mouse-driven invalidate_window calls that would further perturb
queue ordering.

The fix posts one authoritative RefreshStencil + repaint pass after all
dialog windows are fully torn down, ensuring the stencil is current
before any painting happens.

commit.message:
Validate coordinate triplets: require complete sets and reject local/non-local mixing (#354)

Coordinates in commands were validated as independent arguments,
allowing two invalid patterns to pass silently: providing a partial
position set (e.g. `/particle minecraft:endrod 5`) and mixing local
(`^`) with relative/absolute coordinates (e.g. `/particle
minecraft:endrod 1 ^2 ~3`).

## Changes

- **`diagnostics/minecraft/coordinate.ts`** — New
`minecraft_coordinate_set_diagnose` function that scans command
parameter definitions for consecutive `ParameterType.coordinate` runs,
processes them as x/y/z triplets, and emits:
- `minecraft.coordinate.incomplete` — when 1 or 2 of a triplet are
provided but not all 3
- `minecraft.coordinate.mixed` — when local (`^`) coordinates appear
alongside non-local ones in the same triplet
  - No error when the entire optional group is omitted

- **`diagnostics/behavior-pack/mcfunction/commands.ts`** — Calls
`minecraft_coordinate_set_diagnose` after the existing per-parameter
validation loop, covering all commands with coordinate parameters (not
just particle).

- **`test/…/commands/coordinates.test.ts`** — New test file covering
valid cases (full triplets, omitted optional group, mixed
relative/absolute within non-local), and invalid cases (1- or
2-coordinate partials, local/non-local mixing across particle and
setblock commands).
